message:
Next time your cat looks at you from across the room and slowly closes their eyes — then opens them again — pay attention. That lazy, half-asleep blink isn't boredom. It's love. 🐾

Behaviorists actually have a name for it: the "slow blink," or more sweetly, a "kitty kiss." In cat language, hard eye contact is a challenge — so when your cat softens their gaze and slow-blinks at you, they're telling you they feel completely safe. It's one of the clearest ways a cat can say, "I trust you."

Here's the fun part: you can answer back. Look toward your cat, relax your face, and slowly blink. Many cats will blink right back — a tiny, silent little conversation between the two of you. 💛

Try it tonight and tell me — did your cat slow-blink back at you? 😻
